name: Louisa May Alcott
abstract: Louisa May Alcott  ( AWL-kət; November 29, 1832 – March 6, 1888) was an American novelist, short story writer, and poet best known for writing the novel Little Women (1868) and its sequels Good Wives (1869), Little Men (1871), and Jo's Boys (1886). Raised in New England by her transcendentalist parents, Abigail May and Amos Bronson Alcot…

about_quotes.speaker: James Lincoln Collier
about_quotes.evidence: So that was the third book that the family has created by inadvertence.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Lincoln Collier interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007
